name: nml-lang
description: Syntax highlighting and language support for the NML configuration language

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In an untrusted workspace the neutral nml-lsp still validates committed and cached schema (NML is data, not code). A project's own tool language server (`<tool> lsp`) is only launched once you trust the workspace.

walkthroughs.title: Get schema-aware NML editing
walkthroughs.description: Validate NML config against its schema, with completions and hovers.

steps.title: Install the language server
steps.description:
The neutral server is `nml-lsp`. Install it on your PATH (e.g. `cargo install nml-lsp`, or copy it to `~/.cargo/bin/nml-lsp`), or point `nml.server.path` at it.
[Open NML settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22nml.server%22)

steps.title: Use your tool's schema (optional)
steps.description:
If your project is built with a schema-provider tool (e.g. nudge), declare it in `nml-project.nml`:

```
project MyApp:
    provider:
        tool = "nudge"
```

In a trusted workspace the editor then validates against the exact tool binary — zero sync. Otherwise the neutral server serves the tool's published package.

steps.title: Open an NML file
steps.description:
Open any `.nml` file. The status bar (bottom-right) names the active schema and its delivery channel. Errors, completions, and hovers come from that schema.
[Open a folder](command:workbench.action.files.openFolder)

nml.server.path.description: Absolute path to the neutral nml-lsp binary. Defaults to ~/.cargo/bin/nml-lsp. Machine-scoped on purpose: a workspace cannot set it, so a repository can never redirect the language server at an untrusted binary.

scripts.pretest: npm run bundle:wasm && npm run bundle:js && npm run compile
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run bundle:wasm && npm run bundle:js
